description: Pin important messages to a channel so members can quickly find key information without scrolling through history

actors.name: Channel Moderator
actors.description: User with permission to pin and unpin messages in a channel

actors.name: Channel Member
actors.description: Any user with access to the channel who can view pinned messages

actors.name: Messaging System
actors.description: Records pin state and broadcasts changes to channel members

general: Pinning must be enabled at the system/workspace level; if disabled, no pin or unpin actions are permitted
Only users with the pin-message permission for the target channel may pin or unpin messages
The acting user must be a member of the channel and have active access to it
A message that is already pinned is silently returned as-is without raising an error
When a message is pinned, a system notification message is posted in the channel to inform members
End-to-end encrypted messages are pinned using an encrypted system message type to preserve confidentiality
Nested quote attachments in the pinned message are included in the system notification up to the configured chain limit
When unpinning, the user must still be a subscribed member of the channel
If message history retention is enabled, a history clone is saved before unpinning
If the pinned message is the channel's last message, the channel's last-message metadata is updated immediately
Read receipt records are updated to reflect the new pin state when receipt storage is enabled
All pin and unpin actions trigger extensibility hooks so downstream integrations can react

given: system-level message pinning setting is disabled
pinning_disabled.result: Action rejected; pinning is not permitted in this workspace

given: message_id does not correspond to an existing message
message_not_found.result: Action rejected; the target message could not be found

given: channel_id does not correspond to an existing channel
channel_not_found.result: Action rejected; the target channel could not be found

user_not_authenticated.result: Action rejected; user must be logged in

given: authenticated user does not have pin-message permission in the channel
user_not_authorized.result: Action rejected; user lacks the required permission

given: authenticated user cannot access the target channel
user_no_channel_access.result: Action rejected; user does not have access to this channel

given: message is already in pinned state
already_pinned.result: No change made; message was already pinned

given: message exists and belongs to an accessible channel
pin_successful.result: Message is pinned; a system notification appears in the channel

given: user is not subscribed to the channel
unpin_user_not_subscribed.result: Action rejected; only subscribed members may unpin messages

given: message exists and belongs to an accessible channel
user has pin-message permission and is subscribed
unpin_successful.result: Message is unpinned; channel members see the updated pin list

errors.message: Message pinning is not available in this workspace
The message you are trying to pin could not be found
The channel could not be found
You must be logged in to pin messages
You do not have permission to pin messages in this channel
You do not have access to this channel
You must be a member of the channel to unpin messages

events.name: message.pinned
events.description: Fired after a message is successfully pinned to a channel

events.name: message.unpinned
events.description: Fired after a message is successfully unpinned from a channel

related.reason: Pinning applies to channels and direct message threads
Starring is the personal counterpart to channel-level pinning

goals.description: Allow moderators to pin key messages so channel members can quickly locate important information

success_metrics.measurement: Pin/unpin operations performed by authorised users / total pin attempts

human_checkpoints: before disabling the pinning feature workspace-wide

invariants: only users with pin-message permission may pin or unpin messages
pinning is a no-op when the message is already pinned
unpinning requires the user to still be subscribed to the channel

acceptance_tests.given: user without pin-message permission attempts to pin a message

acceptance_tests.expect: no error raised and state unchanged

capabilities.description: Pin and unpin messages within a channel with permission enforcement

tradeoffs.reason: silently ignoring duplicate pin requests prevents noise in integrations that retry
